items.title: Why these retirees won't give up the 3.30am starts and 14-hour shifts

children.content: When the alarm goes off at 3.30am in this freezing outback pit-stop, it's music to the ears of the volunteer bakers who happily spend their winters in a bakery like no other.

Two men carry trays of freshly baked bread loaves up steps from an underground baker.

items.title: Multiple SA voters who 'definitely voted' surprised by failure to vote notices

children.content: Multiple voters have received notices from the Electoral Commission of South Australia informing them they will be fined for not voting in the state election, despite having voted.

image.altText: People lining up to vote outside the Monash Memorial Hall on a sunny day

items.title: Adelaide casino operator fined $21m for 'completely unacceptable' failings

items.title: 'I'm going to stay with you': Woman comes to Modra's aid moments after truck crash

children.content: Linda Cameron describes how she rendered first aid to the former Crows player and held his hand as they waited for an ambulance following a truck crash in South Australia's Fleurieu Peninsula.

children.content: Crows legend Tony Modra has been seriously injured in a truck crash.

articles.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders 'who saved his life'

children.content: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

items.title: Superbikes move leaves Phillip Island community in limbo

items.title: Inquest to probe Adelaide fashion icon's death after using magic mushrooms

children.content: The death of an Adelaide fashion industry icon who suffered a cardiac arrest after taking magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment will be probed at a coronial inquest.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt is photographed outdoors with soft sunlight and greenery in the background.

items.title: 'Sleepwalking into a catastrophe': Proposed data centre in SA raises water use fears

children.content: To its supporters, this proposed data centre will provide much-needed regional jobs, but in the nation's driest state, critics say the draw on river water to cool the machines could be too great.

children.content: A massive data centre proposed for SA faces scrutiny over water and environmental risks.

articles.title: Feud erupts as consumers on the hook for billion-dollar grid blowout

children.content: Transgrid wants to claw back huge cost increases for a major transmission line from consumers. Experts say the regulator's decision will have far-reaching consequences.

items.title: Sarah Game won't rule out introducing another bill to restrict late term abortion

children.content: Family First upper house MLC Sarah Game has not ruled out introducing another bill restricting late-term abortion access, after her proposed legislation to restrict abortion after 25 weeks failed in the lower house last night.

image.altText: A woman in a black dress sits on a red plush couch with wooden desks in front of her.

items.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track livestock lost in floods

items.title: Embattled National Trust of SA to front inquiry over volunteer complaints

items.title: Multi-million-dollar fund for SA farming help not used for a decade

children.content: A South Australian government fund of more than $28 million for loans and grants for farmers has not been used for the past 10 years, despite the industry struggling through drought and poor market conditions.

image.altText: Rows of green vineyards with wooden posts.

items.title: Experts fear algal bloom to blame for disappearance of Whyalla's cuttlefish

children.content: Whyalla is the breeding spot for hundreds of thousands of cuttlefish, but the international spectacle has been cancelled after only dozens have been spotted this year.

items.title: Meloni hits back at Trump's claim she 'begged' him for photo

items.title: Suspected H5 bird flu detected in Australia
